name: Benkei
description: Musashibo Benkei, a towering warrior-monk, stands as a legendary figure in Japanese folklore. Born in the late Heian period, tales paint him as a formidable child prodigy who joined a monastery but soon abandoned religious life for a path of martial prowess. Benkei became renowned for his imposing stature, immense strength, and unwavering loyalty, particularly to Minamoto no Yoshitsune. He famously stood guard on Gojo Bridge in Kyoto, challenging every passing swordsman, defeating 999 until finally losing to Yoshitsune, whom he then pledged to serve. Benkei accompanied Yoshitsune on his many adventures, including the Genpei War, becoming an indispensable member of his retinue. Known for wielding seven weapons simultaneously, including a naginata, axe, and sickle, he was a one-man army. His most famous act of devotion came during Yoshitsune's final stand at Koromogawa, where Benkei, riddled with arrows, remained standing even after death, guarding his lord's body, a testament to his unwavering loyalty and warrior spirit. Benkei embodies the ideals of bushido: loyalty, courage, and self-sacrifice. He is a symbol of strength, unwavering devotion, and the ultimate warrior.
subtitle: Loyal warrior-monk of Japanese legend
achievements: Fighting bravely in the Genpei War
Dying standing up to protect Yoshitsune

keyMyths.title: Benkei on Gojo Bridge
keyMyths.description: Benkei challenged and defeated 999 swordsmen on Gojo Bridge in Kyoto, collecting their swords as trophies. He was finally defeated by Minamoto no Yoshitsune, whom he then swore fealty to.

keyMyths.title: Benkei's Standing Death
keyMyths.description: During Yoshitsune's final stand, Benkei, riddled with arrows, remained standing, protecting Yoshitsune's body. This act symbolized his unwavering loyalty and warrior spirit, a poignant example of Bushido.

keyMyths.title: Benkei's Strength and Loyalty
keyMyths.description: Benkei's immense strength and unwavering loyalty to Yoshitsune are recurring themes in his legends. He represents the ideal of a devoted retainer, willing to sacrifice everything for his lord.
keyMyths.source: Various folk tales and historical accounts

sources.author: Anonymous
sources.description: A Japanese epic account of the Genpei War.
